factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, driven by higher sales and pricing increases.

evidence.text: Sales increased 6.5 percent and 8.6 percent year over year in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, respectively. Organic sales increased 7.1 percent and 8.1 percent year over year in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2022, Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating with significant sales and pricing increases, indicating strong growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with pricing increases contributing to sales growth.

evidence.text: Pricing increased sales in our Intelligent Devices and Software & Control operating segments by approximately 4.5 percentage points and 2.5 percentage points in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2022, Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Pricing increases are contributing to sales growth across key segments, showing pricing power.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with organic sales growth increasing year over year.

evidence.text: Organic sales increased 7.1 percent and 8.1 percent year over year in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2022, Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic sales growth is accelerating, reflecting strong underlying demand.

factors.summary: Revenue growth is driven by real demand and pricing increases, with unit volumes or transaction counts growing.

evidence.text: The results by region, segment, and industry were primarily driven by component availability rather than the underlying demand.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2022, Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Volume growth is mixed due to component availability rather than underlying demand.

factors.summary: End markets such as discrete, process, and hybrid industries are growing fastest, particularly in North America and Latin America.

evidence.text: The increases in North America reported and organic sales in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, compared to the prior periods, were across discrete, process, and hybrid industries.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2022, Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets in key regions are growing fastest, indicating strong market momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, creating margin pressure, particularly in segments like Intelligent Devices and Software & Control.

evidence.text: Segment operating margin decreased to 19.7 percent and 19.5 percent in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, respectively, from 21.9 percent and 21.8 percent in the same periods a year ago, driven by higher input costs and higher investment spend.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs are causing margin pressure in key segments.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to higher input costs and investment spend, despite higher sales and pricing increases.

evidence.text: Segment operating margin decreased to 19.7 percent and 19.5 percent in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2022, respectively, from 21.9 percent and 21.8 percent in the same periods a year ago, driven by higher input costs and higher investment spend, partially offset by higher sales, including pricing increases.
evidence.subsection: Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2022, Compared to Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing due to higher input costs and investment spend.
